fr:mainmatter.fr:p: By the characterization of the externalization we know that the externalization of C so-defined is equivalent to the full subfibration \mathbf {Full} { \mathopen {} \left ( \bar {u} \right ) \mathclose {}} of E spanned by objects that are “classified” by \bar {u}. Because \bar {u} is generic, we know that every object of E is classified by \bar {u}, so we are done.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The internalization of a small fibration

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let E be a small fibration over B a category with finite limits, i.e. a cartesian fibration that is both locally small and globally small. We will show that E is equivalent to the externalization { \mathopen {} \left [ C \right ] \mathclose {}} of an internal category C in B, namely the full internal subcategory associated to the generic object \bar {u} \in E.

By the characterization of the externalization we know that the externalization of C so-defined is equivalent to the full subfibration \mathbf {Full} { \mathopen {} \left ( \bar {u} \right ) \mathclose {}} of E spanned by objects that are “classified” by \bar {u}. Because \bar {u} is generic, we know that every object of E is classified by \bar {u}, so we are done.

The externalization { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} \right ] \mathclose {}} of the internal category { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} associated to a displayed object \bar {u} in a locally small fibered category E over B is equivalent to the full internal subfibration \mathbf {Full} { \mathopen {} \left ( \bar {u} \right ) \mathclose {}}.

We will define a fibred equivalence F : { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} \right ] \mathclose {}} \to \mathbf {Full} { \mathopen {} \left ( \bar {u} \right ) \mathclose {}} over B.

fr:ol.fr:li: Fix x \in B and \chi _{ x } \in { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ x }, i.e. \chi _{ x } : x \to u; we define F { \mathopen {} \left ( \chi _{ x } \right ) \mathclose {}} to be an arbitrary cartesian map \phi _{ x } : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ x } ]{} \bar {u}. (Here we have used the axiom of choice for collections.)

fr:li.fr:p: Fix f : x \to y \in B and \chi _{ x } :x \to u and \chi _{ y } :y \to u and a diagram representing a displayed morphism h from \chi _{ x } to \chi _{ y } over f as below:

We must define F { \mathopen {} \left ( h \right ) \mathclose {}} : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {y}, fixing arbitrary cartesian maps \bar \chi _{ x } : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ x } ]{} \bar {u} and \bar \chi _{ y } : \bar {y} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ y } ]{} \bar {u}. First we lift h:x \to { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ 1 } into E using the universal property of the cartesian lift:

fr:embedded-tex.fr:embedded-tex-body: \begin {tikzpicture}[diagram] \SpliceDiagramSquare <0/>{ height = 1.5cm, west/style = lies over, east/style = lies over, width = 2.5cm, north/node/style = upright desc, ne/style = pullback, north/style = {->,exists}, nw = \bar {x}, sw = x, se = { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ 1 } , ne = { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ 1 } ^{ * } \bar \partial _{ 1 } , south = h, north = \bar {h}, } \SpliceDiagramSquare <1/>{ glue = west, glue target = 0/, height = 1.5cm, width = 2.5cm, north/node/style = upright desc, south/node/style = upright desc, east/style = lies over, ne/style = pullback, ne = \bar \partial _{ 1 } , se = u \times u, south = p, north = \bar {p}, } \SpliceDiagramSquare <2/>{ glue = west, glue target = 1/, height = 1.5cm, width = 2.5cm, north/node/style = upright desc, south/node/style = upright desc, east/style = lies over, ne = \bar {u}, se = u, south = \pi _{ 1 } , north = \bar \pi _{ 1 } , } \draw [->,bend left=30] (0/nw) to node [above] {$ \bar \chi _{ x } $} (2/ne); \draw [->,bend right=30] (0/se) to node [below] {$s$} (2/se); \end {tikzpicture}

fr:li.fr:p: By composition with the “evaluation map” for our hom object, we have a map \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f; \chi _{ y } ]{} \bar {u}:

fr:embedded-tex.fr:embedded-tex-body: \begin {tikzpicture}[diagram] \SpliceDiagramSquare <0/>{ height = 1.5cm, west/style = lies over, east/style = lies over, south/node/style = upright desc, width = 2.5cm, nw = \bar {x}, sw = x, se = { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ 1 } , ne = { \mathopen {} \left [ { \mathopen {} \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle \mathclose {}} \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ 1 } ^{ * } \bar \partial _{ 1 } , south = h, north = \bar {h}, } \SpliceDiagramSquare <1/>{ glue = west, glue target = 0/, height = 1.5cm, width = 2.5cm, south/node/style = upright desc, east/style = lies over, ne/style = pullback, ne = \bar \partial _{ 2 } , se = u \times u, south = p, north = \bar { \epsilon }, } \SpliceDiagramSquare <2/>{ glue = west, glue target = 1/, height = 1.5cm, width = 2.5cm, south/node/style = upright desc, east/style = lies over, ne = \bar {u}, se = u, south = \pi _{ 2 } , north = \bar \pi _{ 2 } , } \draw [->,bend right=30] (0/se) to node [upright desc] {$t$} (2/se); \node (y) [between = 0/sw and 2/se, yshift=-2cm] {$y$}; \draw [bend right=30,->] (0/sw) to node [sloped,below] {$f$} (y); \draw [bend right=30,->] (y) to node [sloped,below] {$ \chi _{ y } $} (2/se); \end {tikzpicture}

fr:li.fr:p: Next we define F { \mathopen {} \left ( h \right ) \mathclose {}} : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {y} using the universal property of (another) cartesian lift:

fr:embedded-tex.fr:embedded-tex-body: \begin {tikzpicture}[diagram] \SpliceDiagramSquare <l/>{ height = 1.5cm, east/style = lies over, west/style = lies over, ne/style = pullback, north/style = {->,exists}, north/node/style = upright desc, ne = \bar {y}, nw = \bar {x}, se = y, sw = x, south = f, north = F { \mathopen {} \left ( h \right ) \mathclose {}} , width = 2.5cm, } \SpliceDiagramSquare <r/>{ glue = west, glue target = l/, height = 1.5cm, east/style = lies over, north/node/style = upright desc, ne = \bar {u}, se = u, south = \chi _{ y } , north = \bar \chi _{ y } , } \draw [->,bend left=30] (l/nw) to node [above] {$ \bar {h}; \bar { \epsilon }; \bar \pi _{ 2 } $} (r/ne); \end {tikzpicture}

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let C be an internal category in E. We may define a fibered category { \mathopen {} \left [ C \right ] \mathclose {}} over E called the externalization of C.

fr:ol.fr:li: Given x \in E, an object of { \mathopen {} \left [ C \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ x } is defined to be a morphism x \to C _{ 0 } in E.

fr:li.fr:p: Given x,y \in E and f:x \to y and u \in { \mathopen {} \left [ C \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ x } and v \in { \mathopen {} \left [ C \right ] \mathclose {}} _{ y }, a morphism u \xrightarrow [ f ]{} v is defined to be a morphism h : x \to C _{ 1 } in E such that the following diagram commutes:
